title: some entries might be missing in `tmpfile.d`
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
`/run/libpod` and `/run/user/*/libpod` might be missing in `tmpfile.d`.
I also don't think, `/var/lib/cni/networks` would touched from `systemd-tmpfiles` at all?

**Describe the results you received:**
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/0a0ade3cc00c3779bbf68ddd103d3efd10b5c25b/contrib/tmpfile/podman.conf#L3-L7

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
x /tmp/podman-run-*
x /tmp/containers-user-*
x /tmp/run-*/libpod
D! /run/lipod 0600 root root
D! /run/podman 0600 root root
D! /run/user/*/libpod
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Actually, I'm not aware, when `systemd-tmpfiles` is executed on all those distributions. On some distributions, like _Debian_ `/tmp/` is NOT _tmpfs_. So the temporary files will survive a reboot (theoretically). I guess on all OS, `/run/` is _tmpfs_, so it won't survive a reboot at all.

My 50 Cent: `systemd-tmpfiles` is highly destructive and *DOES NOT* have a `--dry-run` flag. My god, why???

comments.body:
@mheon 
In commit message https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/9a02b50551d73c1427d158cca85d020fc71e27a7 you also wanted to add `/run/user-$UID/libpod`, but you didn't. Was this just a typo (`s/run/tmp`)?

Ah, it was a typo, but the commit message, not the entry in tmpfiles.d - it
should be in /tmp, not /run. Oops. At least the patch was right, even if
the commit message was off?

As for whether entries in /run should be there - I believe, but am not
sure, that systemd-tmpfiles only targets /tmp (at least, I don't think
we've ever had reports of it wiping our state in /run).

On Sat, Jun 26, 2021 at 6:22 PM tobwen ***@***.***> wrote:

> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon>
> In commit message 9a02b50
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/9a02b50551d73c1427d158cca85d020fc71e27a7>
> you also wanted to add /run/user-$UID/libpod, but you didn't. Was this
> just a typo (s/run/tmp)?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10791#issuecomment-869068359>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCEYDD53O527PAAQQY3TUZHKXANCNFSM47LYCQYQ>
> .
>

> I believe, but am not sure, that systemd-tmpfiles only targets /tmp

I've just seen:
```
 D! /run/podman 0700 root root 
 D! /var/lib/cni/networks 
```

And thought: okay, there might be a reason, why they've done this. But then, it's incomplete :-)

I don't even think, `/var/lib/` will be touched. On Debian, `/var/lib/postgresql/` contains the _PostgreSQL_ clusters. I really, really hope, _systemd-tmpfiles_ will never ever get there.

These lines tell systemd-tmpfiles to delete this content if it exists at boot time.  The /run command is probably unnecessary, since systemd will not boot (As I understand it) if /run is not a tmpfs.
The /var/lib/cni/networks is definitely necessary.

Okay, so:

```
x /tmp/podman-run-*
x /tmp/containers-user-*
x /tmp/run-*/libpod
D! /var/lib/cni/networks 
```

I guess, rootless _cni_ stuff is in _rundir_?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe we are all set, reopen if I am mistaken.
